385	// TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_UsesQueueDepthNotAttemptPromptDepth verifies
386	// that the post-attempt orchestrator checks the queue-level max_decomposition_depth
387	// (from config) and not the implementation-level depth cap (hardcoded 2 in the
388	// execute-bead prompt). A bead at depth 1 with max_decomposition_depth=3 must
389	// still be split even if the rationale mentions the implementation depth cap.
390	func TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_UsesQueueDepthNotAttemptPromptDepth(t *testing.T) {
391		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
392		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
393	
394		// Create bead at depth 1: child of a closed root bead.
395		root := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-qdepth-root", Title: "Root bead", Status: bead.StatusClosed}
396		require.NoError(t, store.Create(root))
397	
398		candidate := &bead.Bead{
399			ID:         "ddx-qdepth-child",
400			Title:      "Child bead at depth 1",
401			Parent:     "ddx-qdepth-root",
402			Acceptance: "1. do the work",
403		}
404		require.NoError(t, store.Create(candidate))
405	
406		var hookCalls int32
407		decomp := &PreClaimDecomposition{
408			Rationale: "orchestrator split at depth 1",
409			Children: []PreClaimDecompositionChild{
410				{Title: "Subtask", Description: "subtask desc", Acceptance: "1. do the work"},
411			},
412			ACMap: []ACMapEntry{
413				{ParentAC: "1. do the work", Coverage: "fully covered by Subtask"},
414			},
415		}
416	
417		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
418			Store: store,
419			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
420				return ExecuteBeadReport{
421					BeadID: beadID,
422					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
423					// Rationale mentions implementation depth cap as a red herring;
424					// the orchestrator must use queue-level max_decomposition_depth (3).
425					NoChangesRationale: "status: open\norchestrator_action: decompose\nreason: implementation depth cap reached at depth 2",
426				}, nil
427			}),
428		}
429	
430		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
431			Assignee:              "worker",
432			MaxDecompositionDepth: 3, // depth 1 < 3, so orchestrator may still split
433		}
434		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
435	
436		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
437			Once:         true,
438			TargetBeadID: "ddx-qdepth-child",
439			PostAttemptDecompositionHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error) {
440				atomic.AddInt32(&hookCalls, 1)
441				return decomp, nil
442			},
443		})
444		require.NoError(t, err)
445		require.NotNil(t, result)
446	
447		// Hook must have been called: depth 1 < max_decomposition_depth 3.
448		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&hookCalls),
449			"PostAttemptDecompositionHook must run: queue depth 1 < max_decomposition_depth 3")
450		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
451		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
452	
453		// Child must be created.
454		all, err := store.ReadAll()
455		require.NoError(t, err)
456		var children []bead.Bead
457		for _, b := range all {
458			if b.Parent == "ddx-qdepth-child" {
459				children = append(children, b)
460			}
461		}
462		assert.Len(t, children, 1, "child must be created when queue depth < max_decomposition_depth")
463	}

491				return ExecuteBeadReport{

507			Once:      true,

532	
533		// decomposition.blocked event must be emitted.
534		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "post_attempt_decomposition.blocked",
535			"blocked event must be emitted for lossy split")
536	}

27	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
28	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
29	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
30	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
31	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
32	// returned to a caller.
33	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
34		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
35		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
36		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
37		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
38		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
39		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
40		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
41		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
42		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
43		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
44		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
45		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
46		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
47		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
48		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
49		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
50	
51		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
52		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
53		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
54		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
55		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
56		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
57		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
58		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
59		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
60		// non-landing outcomes.
61		OrchestratorStatus string `json:"orchestrator_status,omitempty"`
62	
63		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
64		Reason      string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
65		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
66		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
67		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
68		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
69		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
70		CycleIndex          int               `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
71		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
72		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
73		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
74		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
75		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
76		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
77		// generic execution failures.
78		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
79		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
80	
81		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
82		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
83		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
84		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
85	
86		// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent
87		// exited without creating a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt. It helps
88		// operators diagnose silent commit failures before the worktree is cleaned up.
89		NoEvidencePaths []string `json:"no_evidence_paths,omitempty"`
90	
91		// ResourceExhausted captures the root and cleanup summary when execution
92		// stopped before a bead attempt because the host could not safely continue.
93		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
94	
95		Harness                     string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
96		Provider                    string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
97		Model                       string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
98		ActualPower                 int     `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
99		PredictedPower              int     `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
100		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64 `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	// Config holds agent service configuration.
11	type Config struct {
12		Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
21	}
22	
23	// RouteFlags holds raw CLI flag values before normalization into a RouteRequest.
24	// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.
25	type RouteFlags struct {
26		Profile     string // --profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
27		Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin
28		Provider    string // --provider: explicit provider name
29		ModelRef    string // --model-ref: opaque model reference for Fizeau
30		Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
31		Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
32		Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
33	}
34	
35	// RunArgs holds options for a single agent invocation.
36	type RunArgs struct {
37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
41		Context       context.Context
42		Harness       string
43		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
44		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
45		PromptSource  string
46		Correlation   map[string]string
47		Model         string
48		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
49		ModelRef      string // opaque model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved by Fizeau
50		Effort        string
51		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
52		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
53		WorkDir       string
54		Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
55		SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
56		Role          string
57		CorrelationID string
58		Env           map[string]string
59	}
60	
61	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunArgs for the agent
62	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
63	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
64	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
65	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
66	// runtime intent remain.
67	//
68	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
69	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
70		Prompt                string
71		PromptFile            string
72		PromptSource          string
73		Output                io.Writer // optional live service-event progress sink
74		Correlation           map[string]string
75		Record                bool
76		Replay                bool
77		WorkDir               string
78		EstimatedPromptTokens int
79		RequiresTools         bool
80		SessionLogDirOverride string
81		// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, ModelRefOverride, ProfileOverride, and
82		// PermissionsOverride let a caller override one durable knob from rcfg for
83		// this single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
84		// SD-024 step B22d-d: execute-bead sets permissions=unrestricted; hidden
85		// lifecycle calls use profile-only routing hints sourced from fizeau.
86		HarnessOverride     string
87		ModelOverride       string
88		ModelRefOverride    string
89		ProfileOverride     string
90		PermissionsOverride string
91		// ClearRoutingPins drops rcfg Harness/Provider/Model/ModelRef passthrough for
92		// hidden DDx lifecycle calls. Explicit runtime overrides still win.
93		ClearRoutingPins bool
94		Role             string
95		CorrelationID    string
96		Env              map[string]string
97		// MinPowerOverride, when > 0, overrides rcfg.MinPower() for this single
98		// invocation. Used by the post-merge reviewer to pin MinPower above the
99		// implementer's actual selected power, biasing routing toward a stronger
100		// reviewer model (R4 pairing: reviewer >= impl + 1 power).
101		MinPowerOverride int
102		// ClearMinPower drops rcfg.MinPower() for this single invocation. Auxiliary
103		// lifecycle classifier calls should not inherit implementation power bounds;
104		// Fizeau owns routing for those hidden dispatches.
105		ClearMinPower bool
106		// ClearMaxPower drops rcfg.MaxPower() for this single invocation. This is
107		// for auxiliary classifier/reviewer-style calls that deliberately want the
108		// strongest viable route instead of the worker attempt's cost cap.
109		ClearMaxPower bool
110		// ClearProfile drops rcfg.Profile() for this single invocation. Auxiliary
111		// calls select their own fizeau profile hints instead of inheriting the
112		// worker attempt's profile.
113		ClearProfile bool
114	}
115	
116	const (
117		DDXModeEnvKey        = "DDX_MODE"
118		DDXModeBeadExecution = "bead_execution"
119	)
120	
121	// Result holds the output of an agent invocation.
122	type Result struct {
123		Harness                     string  `json:"harness"`
124		Provider                    string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
125		Model                       string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
126		ActualPower                 int     `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
127		PredictedPower              int     `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
128		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64 `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
129		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64 `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
130		PredictedCostSource         string  `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
131		ExitCode                    int     `json:"exit_code"`
132		Output                      string  `json:"output"`
133		CondensedOutput             string  `json:"condensed_output,omitempty"`
134		Stderr                      string  `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
135		// Routing evidence populated by the embedded agent harness (RunAgent) or
136		// the script harness (RunScript). Used by ExecuteBead to record kind:routing
137		// evidence on the bead.
138		RouteReason     string          `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
139		ResolvedBaseURL string          `json:"resolved_base_url,omitempty"`
140		Tokens          int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
141		InputTokens     int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
142		CachedTokens    int             `json:"cached_tokens,omitempty"`
143		OutputTokens    int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
144		CostUSD         float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
145		DurationMS      int             `json:"duration_ms"`
146		Error           string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
147		ToolCalls       []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`       // populated by agent, nil for subprocess
148		AgentSessionID  string          `json:"agent_session_id,omitempty"` // agent session ID for event log cross-reference
149	}
150	
151	// SessionEntry is written to the session log.
152	type SessionEntry struct {
153		ID              string            `json:"id"`
154		Timestamp       time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
155		Harness         string            `json:"harness"`
156		Provider        string            `json:"provider,omitempty"`
157		Surface         string            `json:"surface,omitempty"`
158		CanonicalTarget string            `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
159		BaseURL         string            `json:"base_url,omitempty"`
160		BillingMode     string            `json:"billingMode,omitempty"`
161		Model           string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
162		PromptLen       int               `json:"prompt_len"`
163		Prompt          string            `json:"prompt,omitempty"`
164		PromptSource    string            `json:"prompt_source,omitempty"`
165		Response        string            `json:"response,omitempty"`
166		Correlation     map[string]string `json:"correlation,omitempty"`
167		NativeSessionID string            `json:"native_session_id,omitempty"`
168		NativeLogRef    string            `json:"native_log_ref,omitempty"`
169		TraceID         string            `json:"trace_id,omitempty"`
170		SpanID          string            `json:"span_id,omitempty"`
171		Stderr          string            `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
172		Tokens          int               `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
173		InputTokens     int               `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
174		CachedTokens    int               `json:"cached_tokens,omitempty"`
175		OutputTokens    int               `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
176		CostUSD         float64           `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
177		Duration        int               `json:"duration_ms"`
178		ExitCode        int               `json:"exit_code"`
179		Error           string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
180		TotalTokens     int               `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"` // input + output; populated on every run
181		BaseRev         string            `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`     // git SHA the execution started from (execute-bead only)
182		ResultRev       string            `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`   // git SHA of landed/preserved iteration (execute-bead only)
183		ToolCalls       []ToolCallEntry   `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`   // normalized tool-call stream captured at drain (Story 16)
184	}
185	
186	// ProviderStatus tracks provider connectivity and credit status.
187	type ProviderStatus struct {
188		Reachable bool   `json:"reachable"`
189		CreditsOK bool   `json:"credits_ok,omitempty"` // false if out of credits/quota
190		Error     string `json:"error,omitempty"`
191	}
192	
193	// HarnessCapabilities describes the effective capabilities for a harness.
194	type HarnessCapabilities struct {
195		Harness             string            `json:"harness"`
196		Available           bool              `json:"available"`
197		Binary              string            `json:"binary"`
198		Path                string            `json:"path,omitempty"`
199		Model               string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
200		Models              []string          `json:"models,omitempty"`
201		ReasoningLevels     []string          `json:"reasoning_levels,omitempty"`
202		Surface             string            `json:"surface,omitempty"`          // catalog surface identifier
203		CostClass           string            `json:"cost_class,omitempty"`       // local, cheap, medium, expensive
204		IsLocal             bool              `json:"is_local"`                   // true if embedded/local (no cloud cost)
205		ExactPinSupport     bool              `json:"exact_pin_support"`          // true if harness accepts exact model pin
206		ProfileMappings     map[string]string `json:"profile_mappings,omitempty"` // effective profile → model for this harness
207		SupportsEffort      bool              `json:"supports_effort"`            // true if harness has effort/reasoning flag
208		SupportsPermissions bool              `json:"supports_permissions"`       // true if harness has permission-level flags
209	}
210	
211	// ToolCallEntry records one tool execution during an agent run.
212	// Mirrors the agent library's ToolCallLog without importing it in types.
213	type ToolCallEntry struct {
214		Tool     string `json:"tool"`
215		Input    string `json:"input"`
216		Output   string `json:"output,omitempty"`
217		Duration int    `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
218		Error    string `json:"error,omitempty"`
219	}
220	
221	// ComparisonArm holds the result of one harness arm in a comparison.
222	type ComparisonArm struct {
223		Harness      string          `json:"harness"`
224		Model        string          `json:"model,omitempty"`
225		Output       string          `json:"output"`
226		Diff         string          `json:"diff,omitempty"`         // git diff of side effects
227		ToolCalls    []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`   // agent tool call log (nil for subprocess)
228		PostRunOut   string          `json:"post_run_out,omitempty"` // post-run command output
229		PostRunOK    *bool           `json:"post_run_ok,omitempty"`  // post-run pass/fail
230		Tokens       int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
231		InputTokens  int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
232		OutputTokens int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
233		CostUSD      float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
234		DurationMS   int             `json:"duration_ms"`
235		ExitCode     int             `json:"exit_code"`
236		Error        string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
237	}
238	
239	// ComparisonGrade holds the evaluation of one arm by a grading harness.
240	type ComparisonGrade struct {
241		Arm       string `json:"arm"`
242		Score     int    `json:"score"`
243		MaxScore  int    `json:"max_score"`
244		Pass      bool   `json:"pass"`
245		Rationale string `json:"rationale"`
246	}
247	
248	// ComparisonRecord is the complete record of a comparison run.
249	type ComparisonRecord struct {
250		ID        string            `json:"id"`

252		Prompt    string            `json:"prompt"`
253		Arms      []ComparisonArm   `json:"arms"`
254		Grades    []ComparisonGrade `json:"grades,omitempty"`
255	}
256	
257	// CandidateState captures contract-derived routing state for a candidate.
258	type CandidateState struct {
259		Installed     bool                   `json:"installed"`
260		Reachable     bool                   `json:"reachable"`
261		Authenticated bool                   `json:"authenticated"`
262		QuotaOK       bool                   `json:"quota_ok"`
263		QuotaState    string                 `json:"quota_state,omitempty"` // ok, blocked, unknown
264		Degraded      bool                   `json:"degraded"`
265		PolicyOK      bool                   `json:"policy_ok"`
266		LastChecked   time.Time              `json:"last_checked,omitempty"`
267		Error         string                 `json:"error,omitempty"`
268		Quota         *QuotaInfo             `json:"quota,omitempty"`
269		RoutingSignal *RoutingSignalSnapshot `json:"routing_signal,omitempty"`
270	}
271	
272	// QuotaInfo holds parsed quota data from CLI introspection.
273	type QuotaInfo struct {
274		PercentUsed int    `json:"percent_used"`
275		LimitWindow string `json:"limit_window,omitempty"` // e.g. "5h", "7 day"
276		ResetDate   string `json:"reset_date,omitempty"`   // e.g. "April 12"
277	}
278	
279	// AccountInfo captures provider account metadata from local auth files.
280	type AccountInfo struct {
281		Email    string `json:"email,omitempty"`
282		PlanType string `json:"plan_type,omitempty"`
283		OrgName  string `json:"org_name,omitempty"`
284	}
285	
286	// QuotaWindow captures one quota window (e.g. 5h, weekly, model-specific).
287	type QuotaWindow struct {
288		Name          string  `json:"name"`               // e.g. "5h", "7d", "spark"
289		LimitID       string  `json:"limit_id,omitempty"` // provider limit_id
290		WindowMinutes int     `json:"window_minutes"`
291		UsedPercent   float64 `json:"used_percent"`
292		ResetsAt      string  `json:"resets_at,omitempty"`      // human-readable
293		ResetsAtUnix  int64   `json:"resets_at_unix,omitempty"` // unix timestamp
294		State         string  `json:"state"`
295	}
296	
297	// SignalSourceMetadata captures where a routing signal came from and how
298	// fresh it is. Populated from upstream HarnessInfo/ProviderInfo.
299	type SignalSourceMetadata struct {
300		Provider   string    `json:"provider"`
301		Kind       string    `json:"kind"`
302		Path       string    `json:"path,omitempty"`
303		ObservedAt time.Time `json:"observed_at,omitempty"`
304		Freshness  string    `json:"freshness"`
305		AgeSeconds int64     `json:"age_seconds,omitempty"`
306		Basis      string    `json:"basis,omitempty"`
307		Notes      string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
308	}
309	
310	// NormalizeSignalSourceKind collapses non-live or DDx-local cache labels into
311	// the small vocabulary exposed by the current routing/status surfaces.
312	func NormalizeSignalSourceKind(kind string) string {
313		switch kind {
314		case "native-session-jsonl":
315			return kind
316		default:
317			return ""
318		}
319	}
320	
321	// QuotaSignal captures current quota/headroom for a harness.
322	type QuotaSignal struct {
323		Source        SignalSourceMetadata `json:"source"`
324		State         string               `json:"state"`
325		UsedPercent   int                  `json:"used_percent,omitempty"`
326		WindowMinutes int                  `json:"window_minutes,omitempty"`
327		ResetsAt      string               `json:"resets_at,omitempty"`
328	}
329	
330	// UsageSignal captures token/session totals for a harness.
331	type UsageSignal struct {
332		Source            SignalSourceMetadata `json:"source"`
333		InputTokens       int                  `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
334		CachedInputTokens int                  `json:"cached_input_tokens,omitempty"`
335		OutputTokens      int                  `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
336		TotalTokens       int                  `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
337		SessionCount      int                  `json:"session_count,omitempty"`
338	}
339	
340	// RoutingSignalSnapshot captures routing signal metadata for a harness.
341	// Populated from upstream HarnessInfo/ProviderInfo data — no provider-native
342	// file parsing.
343	type RoutingSignalSnapshot struct {
344		Provider        string               `json:"provider"`
345		Source          SignalSourceMetadata `json:"source"`
346		CurrentQuota    QuotaSignal          `json:"current_quota,omitempty"`
347		HistoricalUsage UsageSignal          `json:"historical_usage,omitempty"`
348		Account         *AccountInfo         `json:"account,omitempty"`
349		QuotaWindows    []QuotaWindow        `json:"quota_windows,omitempty"`
350	}
351	
352	// RoutingOutcome is one bounded sample of DDx-observed routing performance.
353	type RoutingOutcome struct {
354		Harness         string    `json:"harness"`
355		Surface         string    `json:"surface,omitempty"`
356		CanonicalTarget string    `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
357		Model           string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
358		ObservedAt      time.Time `json:"observed_at"`
359		Success         bool      `json:"success"`
360		LatencyMS       int       `json:"latency_ms"`
361		InputTokens     int       `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
362		OutputTokens    int       `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
363		CostUSD         float64   `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
364		NativeSessionID string    `json:"native_session_id,omitempty"`
365		NativeLogRef    string    `json:"native_log_ref,omitempty"`
366		TraceID         string    `json:"trace_id,omitempty"`
367		SpanID          string    `json:"span_id,omitempty"`
368	}
369	
370	// QuotaSnapshot captures one quota/headroom sample for routing.
371	type QuotaSnapshot struct {
372		Harness         string    `json:"harness"`
373		Surface         string    `json:"surface,omitempty"`
374		CanonicalTarget string    `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
375		Source          string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
376		ObservedAt      time.Time `json:"observed_at"`
377		QuotaState      string    `json:"quota_state"`
378		UsedPercent     int       `json:"used_percent,omitempty"`
379		WindowMinutes   int       `json:"window_minutes,omitempty"`
380		ResetsAt        string    `json:"resets_at,omitempty"`
381		SampleKind      string    `json:"sample_kind"`
382	}
383	
384	// BurnSummary is a derived relative subscription-pressure estimate.
385	type BurnSummary struct {
386		Harness         string    `json:"harness"`
387		Surface         string    `json:"surface,omitempty"`
388		CanonicalTarget string    `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
389		ObservedAt      time.Time `json:"observed_at"`
390		BurnIndex       float64   `json:"burn_index"`
391		Trend           string    `json:"trend,omitempty"`
392		Confidence      float64   `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
393		Basis           string    `json:"basis,omitempty"`
394	}
395	
396	// RouteRequest is the normalized routing ask built from CLI flags and config.
397	type RouteRequest struct {
398		Profile         string // default, cheap, fast, smart
399		ModelRef        string // logical model ref or alias resolved by Fizeau
400		ModelPin        string // exact concrete model string passthrough
401		Effort          string // low, medium, high, etc.
402		Permissions     string // safe, supervised, unrestricted
403		HarnessOverride string // forces routing to one harness only
404	}
405	
406	// CandidatePlan is a routing evaluation result for one harness.
407	type CandidatePlan struct {
408		Harness               string         `json:"harness"`
409		Surface               string         `json:"surface,omitempty"`          // Fizeau surface: embedded-openai, embedded-anthropic, codex, claude
410		RequestedRef          string         `json:"requested_ref,omitempty"`    // profile or model ref from the request
411		CanonicalTarget       string         `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"` // resolved upstream canonical target
412		ConcreteModel         string         `json:"concrete_model,omitempty"`   // concrete model string to pass to harness
413		SupportsEffort        bool           `json:"supports_effort"`
414		SupportsPermissions   bool           `json:"supports_permissions"`
415		State                 CandidateState `json:"state"`
416		Provider              string         `json:"provider,omitempty"`            // discovered provider endpoint name (e.g. vidar, bragi)
417		CostClass             string         `json:"cost_class,omitempty"`          // local, cheap, medium, expensive
418		IsSubscription        bool           `json:"is_subscription,omitempty"`     // fixed-subscription harness; preferred over pay-per-token within quota
419		EstimatedCostUSD      float64        `json:"estimated_cost_usd,omitempty"`  // -1 = unknown
420		HistoricalSuccessRate float64        `json:"historical_success_rate"`       // observed success rate from routing metrics; -1 when insufficient data (< 3 samples)
421		RejectReason          string         `json:"reject_reason,omitempty"`       // non-empty means rejected
422		DeprecationWarning    string         `json:"deprecation_warning,omitempty"` // non-empty when requested ref is deprecated
423		Score                 float64        `json:"score,omitempty"`
424		Viable                bool           `json:"viable"`
425	}
426	
427	// Default configuration values.
428	const (
429		DefaultHarness = "codex"
430		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
431		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
432		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
433		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
434		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
435		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
436		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
437		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
438		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
439		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
440		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
441		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
442		// ToolCallTimeout is a per-tool-call sanity cap: if a single tool_call
443		// event has no matching tool_result within this window, the drain cancels
444		// the execution. This is NOT for loop detection (see loopDetector in
445		// agent_runner_service.go) — it catches individually hung subprocesses
446		// (e.g., network deadlock in git push) that would otherwise absorb
447		// unlimited wall-clock without surfacing.
448		ToolCallTimeout = 1800000 // 30 minutes
449		DefaultLogDir   = ".ddx/agent-logs"
450	)
451	
452	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
453	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
454	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
455	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
456	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
457		if configured == "" {
458			configured = DefaultLogDir
459		}
460		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
461			return configured
462		}
463		if projectRoot == "" {
464			return configured
465		}
466		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
467	}
468

22	
23	// beadStrictnessMode returns the strictness mode for review based on bead
24	// labels. kind:doc and kind:mechanical → mechanical; kind:refactor and
25	// kind:chore → behavior-light; default → strict.
26	func beadStrictnessMode(labels []string) string {
27		for _, l := range labels {
28			switch l {
29			case "kind:doc", "kind:mechanical":
30				return StrictnessMechanical
31			case "kind:refactor", "kind:chore":
32				return StrictnessBehavior
33			}
34		}
35		return StrictnessStrict
36	}
37	
38	// strictnessModeInstructions returns the per-mode evidence requirement text
39	// injected into the reviewer prompt.
40	func strictnessModeInstructions(mode string) string {
41		switch mode {
42		case StrictnessMechanical:
43			return "mechanical — file presence, renames, or symbol evidence only; no test-name or runtime evidence required."
44		case StrictnessBehavior:
45			return "behavior-light — build green plus file/symbol evidence suffices; test-name match required only when an AC explicitly names a Test* function."
46		default:
47			return "strict — each AC must be anchored to a named Test* function or a diff-touched symbol; file-only evidence is insufficient."
48		}
49	}
50	
51	// ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind is the event kind appended to a bead
52	// when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CLARIFICATION.
53	const ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind = "review-request-clarification"
54	
55	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
56	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
57	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
58	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
59	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
60	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
61	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
62	
63	// ReviewerEscalatedEventKind is the kind:reviewer-escalated event kind
64	// appended when the reviewer's MinPower is bumped above the baseline
65	// impl.ActualPower+1 due to prior review-error or review-pairing-degraded
66	// events for the same result_rev.
67	const ReviewerEscalatedEventKind = "reviewer-escalated"
68	
69	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
70	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
71	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
72	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
73	type ImplementerRouting struct {
74		Harness     string
75		Provider    string
76		Model       string
77		ActualPower int
78		// Correlation is the implementer's correlation map. The reviewer copies
79		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
80		// aggregations can join the two calls.
81		Correlation map[string]string
82	}
83	
84	// Verdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
85	type Verdict string
86	
87	const (
88		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
89		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
90		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead stays open
91		// and the pre-close gate records the verdict without closing.
92		VerdictRequestChanges Verdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
93		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human
94		// review while remaining open.
95		VerdictBlock Verdict = "BLOCK"
96		// VerdictRequestClarification means the reviewer cannot adjudicate one or
97		// more needs-judgment AC items without additional operator context. Unlike
98		// BLOCK it does not halt automated retry; it parks the bead to
99		// status=proposed so the operator can supply the missing context.
100		VerdictRequestClarification Verdict = "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION"

1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
14		agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can override one knob
34	// for a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			factory := serviceRunFactory
54			if factory == nil {
55				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
56			}
57			built, err := factory(projectRoot)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
60			}
61			svc = built
62		}
63		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
64	}
65	
66	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
67	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
68	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
69	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
70	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
71		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
72		if harness == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
73			harness = rcfg.Harness()
74		}
75		model := runtime.ModelOverride
76		if model == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
77			model = rcfg.Model()
78		}
79		modelRef := runtime.ModelRefOverride
80		if modelRef == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
81			modelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
82		}
83		provider := ""
84		if !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
85			provider = rcfg.Provider()
86		}
87		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
88		if permissions == "" {
89			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
90		}
91		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
92		if sessionLogDir == "" {
93			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
94		}
95	
96		var opts RunArgs
97		opts.Context = ctx
98		opts.Harness = harness
99		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
100		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
101		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
102		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
103		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
104		opts.Role = runtime.Role
105		opts.CorrelationID = runtime.CorrelationID
106		opts.Env = runtime.Env
107		opts.Model = model
108		opts.Provider = provider
109		opts.ModelRef = modelRef
110		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
111		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
112		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
113		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
114		opts.Permissions = permissions
115		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
116		return opts
117	}

1	// Package executeloop defines the canonical spec type for execute-loop workers.
2	// It is import-safe: no dependency on cobra, server, or transport packages.
3	package executeloop
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"strconv"
9		"time"
10	)
11	
12	// SpecCurrentVersion is the single spec version supported by this client and
13	// server. Forward-compat stance: a client MUST set SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion;
14	// a server receiving an unknown version SHOULD reject with an error rather than
15	// silently misinterpreting fields. There is no backwards-compat shim — upgrade
16	// client and server together.
17	const SpecCurrentVersion = 1
18	
19	// Mode controls how the execute-loop terminates.
20	type Mode string
21	
22	const (
23		// ModeOnce runs one bead (or none if the queue is empty) then exits.
24		ModeOnce Mode = "once"
25		// ModeDrain processes all ready beads then exits.
26		ModeDrain Mode = "drain"
27		// ModeWatch processes all ready beads, then sleeps for IdleInterval and
28		// rechecks, looping indefinitely until interrupted.
29		ModeWatch Mode = "watch"
30	)
31	
32	// Duration wraps time.Duration with JSON support that marshals as a string
33	// (e.g., "30s") and unmarshals both strings ("30s") and numeric nanoseconds.
34	type Duration struct {
35		time.Duration
36	}
37	
38	func (d Duration) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
39		return json.Marshal(d.String())
40	}
41	
42	func (d *Duration) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
43		// Try string first.
44		var s string
45		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err == nil {
46			parsed, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
47			if err != nil {
48				return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: invalid duration string %q: %w", s, err)
49			}
50			d.Duration = parsed
51			return nil
52		}
53		// Try numeric nanoseconds.
54		var ns int64
55		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &ns); err == nil {
56			d.Duration = time.Duration(ns)
57			return nil
58		}
59		return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: cannot unmarshal %s as duration (want string or nanosecond integer)", strconv.Quote(string(b)))
60	}
61	
62	// ExecuteLoopSpec is the canonical, transport-agnostic specification for an
63	// execute-loop worker. All transports (cobra, REST, GraphQL) map into this type
64	// before persisting or dispatching.
65	type ExecuteLoopSpec struct {
66		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root,omitempty"`
67		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
68		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
69		Profile     string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
70		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
71		ModelRef    string `json:"model_ref,omitempty"`
72		Effort      string `json:"effort,omitempty"`
73		LabelFilter string `json:"label_filter,omitempty"`
74	
75		// Mode controls loop termination. Defaults to ModeDrain via ApplyDefaults.
76		Mode Mode `json:"mode,omitempty"`
77	
78		// IdleInterval is the sleep duration between queue polls when Mode=watch
79		// and no execution-ready work is found.
80		IdleInterval Duration `json:"idle_interval,omitempty"`
81	
82		NoReview      bool   `json:"no_review,omitempty"`
83		ReviewHarness string `json:"review_harness,omitempty"`
84		ReviewModel   string `json:"review_model,omitempty"`
85	
86		// OpaquePassthrough skips DDx-side route validation and config
87		// harness/model injection (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010).
88		OpaquePassthrough bool `json:"opaque_passthrough,omitempty"`
89	
90		MaxCostUSD       float64  `json:"max_cost_usd,omitempty"`
91		MaxBeadCostUSD   float64  `json:"max_bead_cost_usd,omitempty"`
92		RequestTimeout   Duration `json:"request_timeout,omitempty"`
93		RateLimitMaxWait Duration `json:"rate_limit_max_wait,omitempty"`
94		MinPower         int      `json:"min_power,omitempty"`
95		MaxPower         int      `json:"max_power,omitempty"`
96	
97		// FromRev, if set, narrows execution to beads introduced after this git revision.
98		FromRev string `json:"from_rev,omitempty"`
99	
100		// SpecVersion must equal SpecCurrentVersion. See SpecCurrentVersion for the
101		// client/server forward-compat stance.
102		SpecVersion int `json:"spec_version,omitempty"`
103	}
104	
105	// DispatchOptions carries control-plane fields used at dispatch time. It is not
106	// persisted into the worker record.
107	type DispatchOptions struct {
108		// Local, when true, runs the worker in-process rather than via the server API.
109		Local bool `json:"local,omitempty"`
110		// JSON is the raw JSON payload to forward when dispatching via REST/GraphQL.
111		// Callers that build the request body manually may set this instead of
112		// populating an ExecuteLoopSpec.
113		JSON string `json:"json,omitempty"`
114	}
115	
116	// ApplyDefaults fills in zero-value fields with their canonical defaults.
117	// Safe to call on a zero-value spec.
118	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) ApplyDefaults() {
119		if s.Mode == "" {
120			s.Mode = ModeDrain
121		}
122		if s.Mode == ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration == 0 {
123			s.IdleInterval = Duration{30 * time.Second}
124		}
125		if s.SpecVersion == 0 {
126			s.SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion
127		}
128	}
129	
130	// Validate checks that the spec is well-formed after ApplyDefaults has run.
131	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) Validate() error {
132		switch s.Mode {
133		case ModeOnce, ModeDrain, ModeWatch:
134		default:
135			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unknown mode %q (want once, drain, or watch)", s.Mode)
136		}
137		if s.Mode != ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration != 0 {
138			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: idle_interval is only valid when mode=watch")
139		}
140		if s.SpecVersion != 0 && s.SpecVersion != SpecCurrentVersion {
141			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unsupported spec_version %d (want %d)", s.SpecVersion, SpecCurrentVersion)
142		}
143		return nil
144	}

input.content:
# Decomposer Agent Prompt

## Role

You are the DDx bead decomposer. You receive a bead that has been flagged as too large for a single
execution attempt and its failure history. Your task is to break it into 2–5 independently
executable child beads.

## Input Contract

You will receive a JSON object with:

- `bead`: the original bead body, including `id`, `title`, `description`, `acceptance`, and `labels`
- `failure_history`: an array of recent execution events (kind + summary) that explain why the bead
  was flagged as too large

## Output Contract

Return **exactly one JSON array** of 2–5 child spec objects. Each object must have these fields:

```json
[
  {
    "title": "Imperative verb phrase naming the subsystem and change",
    "description": "Full problem statement with root cause, proposed fix, and non-scope. Must be self-contained — no references to /tmp or chat context.",
    "acceptance": "1. First AC\n2. Second AC\n3. cd cli && go test ./... green\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes",
    "labels": ["phase:N", "area:foo", "kind:feature"]
  }
]
```

Field constraints:
- `title` (string, required): imperative, names subsystem and change
- `description` (string, required): full problem context; must not reference chat history or temp paths
- `acceptance` (string, required): numbered list with 3–6 ACs; last two must be test gate + pre-commit gate
- `labels` (array of strings, required): copy parent labels; adjust phase/area/kind as needed

## Role Guidance

Each child bead must satisfy all of the following:

1. **Independently executable**: an agent given only the child bead body (no operator context) can
   pick a file to edit and run tests without asking questions.
2. **Atomic scope**: ~3–6 acceptance criteria, no mixing of design, implementation, integration, and
   docs as separate top-level deliverables.
3. **Explicit deps**: if ordering is required between children, say so in the description with
   "DEPENDS ON: <title of sibling>" and the parent will wire dep edges after you return.
4. **No scope invention**: do not invent acceptance criteria that were not present or implied in the
   parent bead's description and acceptance.
5. **Wired-in assertion**: at least one AC must name a specific `Test*` function or `go test -run`
   filter.

## What not to do

- Do not return prose, markdown headers, or explanation outside the JSON array.
- Do not return a JSON object with a `children` key — return the bare array.
- Do not return fewer than 2 or more than 5 children.
- Do not leave `title`, `description`, or `acceptance` empty or whitespace-only.

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// RecoveryFailureClass categorises the persistent failure mode that exhausted
9	// the escalation ladder, so the PostLadderExhaustionHook can choose the
10	// appropriate recovery action.
11	type RecoveryFailureClass string
12	
13	const (
14		// SpecGap is set when the last attempt was blocked by a spec-gap or
15		// missing-acceptance review verdict.
16		SpecGap RecoveryFailureClass = "spec_gap"
17		// TooLarge is set when the last attempt was blocked by a too-large review
18		// verdict.
19		TooLarge RecoveryFailureClass = "too_large"
20		// PersistentExecutionFailed is the default class for all other persistent
21		// failures.
22		PersistentExecutionFailed RecoveryFailureClass = "persistent_execution_failed"
23	)
24	
25	// RecoveryPath indicates which automated recovery action a
26	// PostLadderExhaustionHook took.
27	type RecoveryPath string
28	
29	const (
30		Reframe   RecoveryPath = "reframe"
31		Decompose RecoveryPath = "decompose"
32	)
33	
34	// PostLadderExhaustionResult is the outcome returned by a
35	// PostLadderExhaustionHook invocation.
36	type PostLadderExhaustionResult struct {
37		Attempted bool
38		Succeeded bool
39		Path      RecoveryPath
40		CostUSD   float64
41	}
42	
43	// PostLadderExhaustionHook is called when the consecutive_ladder_exhaustions
44	// counter for a bead reaches the auto-recovery threshold (>= 2). It should
45	// attempt automated recovery and return the outcome. A nil hook or a result
46	// with Attempted=false causes the caller to fall through to the existing loop
47	// path unchanged.
48	type PostLadderExhaustionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, failureClass RecoveryFailureClass) (*PostLadderExhaustionResult, error)
49	
50	// deriveRecoveryFailureClass maps the last-attempt report to a
51	// RecoveryFailureClass for use by the PostLadderExhaustionHook.
52	func deriveRecoveryFailureClass(report ExecuteBeadReport) RecoveryFailureClass {
53		switch {
54		case strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap),
55			strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance):
56			return SpecGap
57		case strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge):
58			return TooLarge
59		default:
60			return PersistentExecutionFailed
61		}
62	}

1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
14		agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can override one knob
34	// for a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			factory := serviceRunFactory
54			if factory == nil {
55				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
56			}
57			built, err := factory(projectRoot)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
60			}
61			svc = built
62		}
63		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
64	}
65	
66	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
67	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
68	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
69	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
70	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
71		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
72		if harness == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
73			harness = rcfg.Harness()
74		}
75		model := runtime.ModelOverride
76		if model == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
77			model = rcfg.Model()
78		}
79		modelRef := runtime.ModelRefOverride
80		if modelRef == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
81			modelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
82		}
83		provider := ""
84		if !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
85			provider = rcfg.Provider()
86		}
87		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
88		if permissions == "" {
89			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
90		}
91		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
92		if sessionLogDir == "" {
93			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
94		}
95	
96		var opts RunArgs
97		opts.Context = ctx
98		opts.Harness = harness
99		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
100		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
101		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
102		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
103		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
104		opts.Role = runtime.Role
105		opts.CorrelationID = runtime.CorrelationID
106		opts.Env = runtime.Env
107		opts.Model = model
108		opts.Provider = provider
109		opts.ModelRef = modelRef
110		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
111		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
112		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
113		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
114		opts.Permissions = permissions
115		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
116		return opts
117	}
